title: BharatSearch: The Knowledge Utility
subtitle: A Zero-to-One Vernacular Search Engine for India's Next Billion Users
problem_statement: Despite 700 million internet users in India, 80% of the population feels alienated by current search engines that prioritize English-centric, text-heavy architectures. The 'Digital Divide' has evolved into a 'Cognitive Divide' where users in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities struggle with complex input methods, misinformation, and lack of localized context, leading to high abandonment rates and reliance on unverified social media clusters for information.

pillars.title: Language Familiarity Bias
pillars.insight: Users experience 'keyboard anxiety' when forced to use QWERTY layouts for native phonetic scripts, leading to short, ineffective queries.

pillars.title: Audio-Visual Dependency
pillars.insight: New-to-internet users trust video and audio content 3x more than written articles, viewing multimedia as a proxy for human interaction.

pillars.title: Verification Seeking
pillars.insight: Indian users frequently cross-check web information within WhatsApp circles, indicating a need for 'Social Proof' in search results.

artifacts.label: Strategy
artifacts.title: Defining the Challenges
questions: How might we enable search for users who cannot or prefer not to type in English?
How might we transform flat search results into actionable localized tasks?
How might we verify the credibility of regional content to build digital trust?

personas.name: Asha Rani
personas.quote: I want to grow my business but most websites are too complicated to read in a hurry.
personas.bio: Asha uses the internet primarily for WhatsApp and YouTube. She wants to source fabrics and find modern designs but finds typing in Hindi/English difficult on a small screen.

points.action: Replace traditional blue links with interactive cards and video summaries
points.benefit: Easier to understand for people who prefer watching over reading

points.action: Merge search results with a direct WhatsApp chat button for local businesses
points.benefit: Lets users talk directly to experts instead of just reading about them

points.action: Create an offline-first mode that saves search results for later viewing
points.benefit: Helps users who have spotty internet connections in rural areas

points.action: Add a 'Trust Meter' badge next to government and health information
points.benefit: Reduces fear of fake news and builds confidence in the app

pivot.before: A global search engine indexing the world's text for desktop users.
pivot.pivot_logic: Shift from a 'Keyword-Index' model to a 'Task-Multimodal' model for Bharat.
pivot.after: A vernacular-first discovery engine that uses voice, visuals, and community trust to solve local problems.

stages.actions: Deciding what to look for (e.g., government schemes)
stages.friction: Uncertainty on which keywords to use in English.

stages.actions: Attempts to type or use voice search
stages.friction: Voice recognition fails on local dialects or accents.
Cognitive overload from text-dense snippets and ads.

stages.actions: Attempting to fulfill the need (e.g., applying for a loan)
stages.friction: Landing pages are not mobile-optimized or in regional languages.

annotations.title: Persistent Voice Anchor
annotations.rationale: A large, central microphone button eliminates the need to navigate to a search bar, catering to low-literacy users.

annotations.title: Visual Topic Bubbles
annotations.rationale: Using icons and regional text categories reduces cognitive load compared to a blank white screen.

annotations.title: Verification Badge
annotations.rationale: Clear green checkmarks on results indicate government-verified sources to combat local misinformation.
